title: Weaponizing Cricket: How Geopolitics Hijacked South Asian Sports
content:
The sport of cricket has increasingly become entangled in the complex geopolitics of South Asia. Like all teams except England and Australia, the Pakistan cricket board heavily relies on funding from the International Cricket Council. However, its government recently calculated that upsetting India’s political establishment was worth potential financial and sporting losses. This tension is exacerbated by the fact that India’s powerful cricket board acts almost as a wing of the ruling political party. Consequently, the leadership of these national cricket boards is deeply tied to their respective government ministries.

Observers seeking to understand South Asia’s dysfunctional international relationships could easily be overwhelmed by the region's historical grievances. Decades of wars, coups, and terrorism are superimposed upon centuries of deep-seated religious and linguistic divides. For many onlookers, observing the dynamics of international cricket provides a clearer lens into these geopolitical realities. While the rules of the sport pursue a clear victory, the regional geopolitics often resemble a negative-sum game. Ultimately, the intense rivalries played out on the cricket pitch perfectly mirror the broader diplomatic brinkmanship between these nations.
